text.title: Tool-Specific Dashboard Configuration
text.summary: Widget configurations for Jira gadgets, Linear views, ClickUp widgets, and portfolio dashboards.

rule.statement: Define the metric schema in a config file before building widgets; validate every query manually three times with real data before scheduling automated delivery.
rule.rationale: Metric definitions that change after widgets are built break all dependent dashboards simultaneously; manual validation catches JQL/filter errors before they produce misleading automated reports.

antipattern.description: Building widgets first and defining what they measure later.
antipattern.reason: Widget reconfiguration after team adoption requires re-education and creates inconsistent historical data.

rule.statement: Use these core Jira gadgets: Sprint Burndown (agile-burndown-chart), Sprint Health, Created vs Resolved (2D filter), Priority distribution (pie chart), and a Filter Results table for personal open issues.
rule.rationale: These five gadgets answer the four PM questions: how is the sprint progressing, what is the quality trend, what is the workload, and what are my personal actions.

rule.statement: Use Linear's built-in Insights for cycle velocity, issue completion, bug count, and team workload — do not try to replicate these with external tools.
rule.rationale: Linear computes these metrics from its internal data model; external queries miss in-progress partial completions and cycle rollover handling.

rule.statement: Portfolio dashboards must include: status overview (all projects, health indicator), delivery confidence (on-time %), resource utilization, burnup chart (quarterly scope), and risk summary (top 5 by impact).
rule.rationale: Executive stakeholders need a single screen that answers: are we on track, are resources sufficient, and what risks require escalation — without opening individual project boards.
